visual_features.overall_style: Traditional, corporate legal aesthetic with dark, formal colors (black, deep red, gold) and a strong, authoritative tone. The design leans more classic than modern, using bold all-caps navigation and a dramatic courthouse hero image to convey gravitas and trust.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by color contrast, size, and position. The brand/logo in gold on a black background is the first anchor, followed by the navigation bar in deep red. In the hero, the large white tagline over the darkened courthouse image creates a strong focal point, with the bright cyan CTAs immediately below acting as the primary actionable focus. Secondary information such as office locations (orange/gold) and practice areas at the bottom are handled with smaller text and lower contrast.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate whitespace; the header and nav are fairly compact and dense, but the hero section has more breathing room around the tagline and the two CTAs. Padding around nav items is sufficient for click targets but not overly generous, supporting a more information-dense, traditional corporate look rather than a minimalistic one.
unique_elements: Gold laurel + scales-of-justice logo on pure black background
Full-width deep red navigation bar immediately under a black contact/brand bar
Hero section with architectural courthouse columns and subtle darkening to support white overlay text
Twin side-by-side primary CTAs (USA vs Jamaica) in bright cyan for clear jurisdictional choices
Bottom strip with inline practice areas separated by centered bullets and styled in gold text

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with content centered within a max-width container. Navigation uses horizontal flex-like distribution; hero content appears centered within the central portion of the grid.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic top-aligned header + full-width navigation + full-width hero banner. No sidebars are visible; content is stacked vertically in full-width sections.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: (1) Black header strip with logo on the left and office hours/contact info on the right; (2) Deep red horizontal navigation bar with primary menu items; (3) Full-width hero image with overlay text (tagline and office/location descriptor) and two central CTA buttons; (4) A dark translucent band at the bottom listing practice areas inline.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header content is horizontally distributed: logo left, contact info right, both vertically centered. Navigation links are horizontally centered in a row and evenly spaced with consistent left/right padding. Hero text and CTAs are center-aligned both horizontally and vertically within the hero area. Bottom practice areas are centered text across the width.
responsive_hints: Navigation uses single-line horizontal layout; will likely collapse into a hamburger or stacked menu on smaller screens.
Hero content is centrally aligned, suggesting it can reflow into stacked vertical order (headline, subtext, then CTAs) on mobile.
Twin CTAs are currently side-by-side; likely to stack vertically on small viewports for readability and tap targets.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: a compact top utility/header bar, followed immediately by a full-width horizontal navigation; then a large hero section with a background image. Within the hero, there is a vertical stack: primary tagline (two-line headline), secondary location statement beneath, then a horizontal pair of CTAs centered in the middle of the hero. At the very bottom of the hero, a full-width narrow strip lists practice areas in a single line with bullets.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The top black header and the red nav together occupy roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport. The hero image and its overlay content occupy the next ~60–70%, creating a dominant central visual region. The bottom practice-areas strip is thin (about 5–10% of the height). Within the hero, the buttons and text occupy the central horizontal band, leaving substantial vertical space above and below for the architectural image.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately dense. The header and nav are packed with information and tightly arranged, typical of professional service sites that prioritize information over minimalist aesthetics. The hero’s central content has more spacing and larger type, but overall the design favors clear, direct communication over airy, editorial-style whitespace.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye typically follows a top-down Z-pattern: starting at the logo on the top-left, moving across to the hours/phone numbers on the top-right, then down to the centered navigation bar. From there, the visual weight of the large white tagline on the darker hero image pulls attention down toward the middle of the page, and then further down to the highly saturated cyan CTAs. Finally, the eye reaches the bottom strip listing practice areas, which acts as a subtle footer-like anchor for the hero.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Bright cyan rectangular buttons with slightly rounded corners (approx. 4–6px radius). Full-width background fill, no visible border; white uppercase text centered both horizontally and vertically. Button height is relatively tall (around 48–56px on desktop) with generous left/right padding, giving them strong presence against the muted background image. Flat design (no gradient or strong shadow); emphasis relies on color contrast and size.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Likely implemented as elements styled as buttons, grouped inside a flex container for side-by-side alignment on desktop. Typical structure:
Schedule a Consultation USASchedule a Consultation Jamaica
.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Not explicitly visible in this screenshot. However, the hero section elements behave like a pseudo-card overlay: text and buttons form a content block floating over the image without borders or shadows. Any cards elsewhere on the site would likely follow the same flat, rectangular aesthetic, likely with thin borders or subtle shadow and small corner radius (2–4px), adhering to the formal brand style.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Expected pattern:

Title

Body text...

Action
, possibly in a grid or column layout.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form elements are not directly visible here. Given the rest of the styling, inputs are likely full-width rectangular fields with 2–4px radius, a light border (possibly #CCCCCC or a muted neutral), and white or very light background. Labels likely appear above fields in sentence case, with error messages in red text below fields. Overall density probably moderate, matching the businesslike tone (compact but legible).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Likely stacked labels with full-width inputs inside a vertical form layout:
...
. Sections may be grouped using headings rather than fieldsets, but fieldsets could be used for multi-part forms (e.g., contact vs case details).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Full-width horizontal navigation bar with deep red background. Menu items are uppercase sans-serif text, evenly spaced with generous horizontal padding. Text is white, with likely hover state changing text color to gold or underlining. The active page may use a different text color (gold) or slightly different background shade. The overall character is strong, formal, and flat (no gradients, minimal dividers).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typical implementation as an unordered list:
  • Home
    • ...
. Items are horizontally laid out using flex or inline-block, with the nav spanning 100% width just below the top header bar. Some items (like 'Schedule Consultation') may be visually emphasized but remain text links rather than button-styled elements in this bar.

ui_patterns.visual_description: At the bottom of the hero, practice areas are presented as an inline text list separated by centered bullets (•). Text is gold on a dark background, medium size, and fairly close together, forming a dense but readable strip. No visible list markers or borders; separation is entirely typographic.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Likely simple inline text or inline list items. Possible structures: (1) a single

with spans and bullet characters; or (2)

  • Florida • Immigration, Family Law, Personal Injury
  • Jamaica • Probate, Real Estate, Family, Litigation
styled with display:inline or inline-block to appear in one line.

heading.visual_style: Brand name appears in a classic, high-contrast serif (Trajan-like) conveying tradition and authority. Hero headlines and nav items look like a bold, clean sans-serif with simple forms and medium x-height.
heading.recommended: Cinzel for the logo/brand and key headings, paired with DM Sans or Space Grotesk for navigation and hero headlines.

body.visual_style: Neutral, readable sans-serif body copy, slightly condensed in nav and contact text but overall straightforward and unobtrusive.
body.recommended: Source Sans 3 or Libre Franklin for body text and general UI copy to balance readability with a modern but not overly trendy look.

font_families.code: Not applicable for this legal marketing site; if needed, use 'Source Code Pro, monospace'.

font_sizes.h1: 36–40px on desktop (used for primary hero tagline)
font_sizes.h3: 22–24px (sub-headings, emphasized lines like 'Offices in Florida & Jamaica')
font_sizes.body: 15–16px (navigation items, body copy, practice areas strip)

spacing_system.component_padding: Navigation items: ~12–16px horizontal padding, 8–10px vertical. Buttons: ~24–32px horizontal, 12–16px vertical. Header content (logo and contact area) has ~16–24px padding from the viewport edges.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between nav and hero text block) is around 24–32px; hero top/bottom padding around main content cluster is ~48–64px to let the background image breathe.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely change text color (from white to gold) or show an underline on hover; active state may persist with gold or slightly different background.
Primary cyan CTAs likely darken or lighten slightly on hover with a smooth 150–250ms ease-out background-color transition; cursor changes to pointer.
Links in body text or practice areas may change from gold to white or underline on hover, reinforcing interactivity.

Subtle fade-in or parallax on the hero image could be added without breaking the formal tone, but any animation should be minimal and smooth to preserve professionalism (e.g., 200–300ms fades, no bouncy easing).
